title: ToolHTTPInvoke
description: The tool-http.v1 INVOKE envelope (spec §28.24): a control-plane -> remote-tool-server HTTP transport message, NOT an engine frame or command kind (the engine only ever sees tool.request/tool.result; the remote path lives entirely inside the broker's dispatch). The request is signed with the tool revision's shared secret over the standard-webhooks input `v1.<Webhook-Id>.<Webhook-Timestamp>.<raw-body>` (HMAC-SHA-256, constant-time verify, timestamp replay window); the Webhook-Id header carries the tool_call_id, which also rides as `Idempotency-Key` so a duplicate retry settles ONE execution server-side. A server answers 200 with the result inline, or 202 to deliver it later by POSTing the result callback (tool-http-callback.json) to `callback.url` bearing the one-use `callback.token`. The T9 SDK verifier implements this SAME signed input verbatim.
